You are an expert C/C++ software engineer working on the mbed-os codebase.
ARM Mbed OS is an embedded operating system for ARM Cortex-M microcontrollers. It includes a hardware abstraction layer (HAL), RTOS primitives, peripheral drivers (SPI, I2C, UART, CAN, USB), connectivity (BLE, Wi-Fi, cellular), storage, and security. C/C++ codebase.

TOOL USAGE — you MUST use these exact formats:

To run a shell command:
<bash>your_command_here</bash>

To view a file:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>

To view specific lines:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor> [start_line, end_line]

To edit a file (replace text):
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>exact text to replace</old_str>
<new_str>replacement text</new_str>

When finished, say: SUBMIT

IMPORTANT: Each response must contain EXACTLY ONE tool call. Do NOT put multiple tool calls in one response.

Working directory is /repo.
You will be given a PR description. Implement the described changes.

Please implement the following pull request:

## Title: Add NULL checks for EGU instance in SWI driver to prevent crashes

### Summary
This PR fixes a potential NULL pointer dereference in the nrfx SWI driver. The `nrfx_swi_egu_instance_get()` function can return NULL for certain SWI instances, but this return value was not being checked before passing it to EGU functions like `nrf_egu_int_enable()` and `nrf_egu_int_disable()`. This could lead to crashes or undefined behavior when these functions are called with a NULL pointer.

The issue was identified in three functions within `nrfx_swi.c`:
1. `swi_handler_setup()` - Missing NULL check before calling `nrf_egu_int_enable()`
2. `nrfx_swi_all_free()` - Missing NULL check before calling `nrf_egu_int_disable()`
3. `egu_irq_handler()` - Missing NULL check before using the EGU instance

The fix adds proper NULL checks around EGU function calls, conditional on the compile-time check `(NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT < SWI_COUNT)`, matching the existing pattern used in `nrfx_swi_trigger()`.

### Changes
- `targets/TARGET_NORDIC/TARGET_NRF5x/TARGET_SDK_15_0/modules/nrfx/drivers/src/nrfx_swi.c`: 
  - Added NULL check in `swi_handler_setup()` before calling `nrf_egu_int_enable()`
  - Added NULL check in `nrfx_swi_all_free()` before calling `nrf_egu_int_disable()`
  - Added NULL check in `egu_irq_handler()` with early return to prevent NULL dereference

### Testing
Code review and static analysis. The fix follows the existing pattern already used in the same file for similar cases.

    42	
    43	#if NRFX_CHECK(NRFX_SWI_ENABLED)
    44	
    45	#include <nrfx_swi.h>
    46	
    47	#define NRFX_LOG_MODULE SWI
    48	#include <nrfx_log.h>

    51	// NRFX_SWI_RESERVED_MASK - SWIs reserved for use by external modules.
    52	#if NRFX_CHECK(NRFX_PWM_NRF52_ANOMALY_109_WORKAROUND_ENABLED)
    53	#define NRFX_SWI_RESERVED_MASK  ((NRFX_SWI_USED) | \

    56	#define NRFX_SWI_RESERVED_MASK  (NRFX_SWI_USED)
    57	#endif
    58	
    59	// NRFX_SWI_DISABLED_MASK - SWIs excluded from use in <nrfx_config.h>.
    60	#if NRFX_CHECK(NRFX_SWI0_DISABLED)
    61	#define NRFX_SWI0_DISABLED_MASK (1u << 0)
    62	#else
    63	#define NRFX_SWI0_DISABLED_MASK 0u
    64	#endif
    65	#if NRFX_CHECK(NRFX_SWI1_DISABLED)

    67	#else
    68	#define NRFX_SWI1_DISABLED_MASK 0u
    69	#endif
    70	#if NRFX_CHECK(NRFX_SWI2_DISABLED)
    71	#define NRFX_SWI2_DISABLED_MASK (1u << 2)
    72	#else

    74	#endif

    79	#endif

    89	#endif
    90	#define NRFX_SWI_DISABLED_MASK  (NRFX_SWI0_DISABLED_
... [truncated] ...
01	// NRFX_SWI_AVAILABLE_MASK - SWIs available for this module, i.e. present
   102	// in the hardware and neither reserved by external modules nor disabled
   103	// in <nrfx_config.h>.
   104	#define NRFX_SWI_PRESENT_MASK   ((1u << (SWI_COUNT)) - 1u)
   105	#define NRFX_SWI_AVAILABLE_MASK (NRFX_SWI_PRESENT_MASK &    \
   106	                                 ~(NRFX_SWI_RESERVED_MASK | \
   107	                                   NRFX_SWI_DISABLED_MASK))
   108	
   109	#if (NRFX_SWI_AVAILABLE_MASK == 0)
   110	#error "No available SWI instances. Check <nrfx_config.h> and NRFX_SWI_USED."
   111	#endif
   112	
   113	#define NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(idx)  ((NRFX_SWI_AVAILABLE_MASK >> (idx)) & 1u)
   114	
   115	#define NRFX_SWI_FIRST  (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(0) ? 0u : \
   116	                        (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(1) ? 1u : \
   117	                        (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(2) ? 2u : \
   118	                        (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(3) ? 3u : \
   119	                        (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(4) ? 4u : \
   120	                                                    5u)))))
   121	#define NRFX_SWI_LAST   (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(5) ? 5u : \
   122	                        (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(4) ? 4u : \
   123	                        (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(3) ? 3u : \
   124	                        (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(2) ? 2u : \
   125	                        (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(1) ? 1u : \
   126	                                                    0u)))))
   127	
   128	// NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT - number of EGU instances to be used by this module
   129	// (note - if EGU is not present, EGU_COUNT is not defined).
   130	#if NRFX_CHECK(NRFX_EGU_ENABLED)
   131	#define NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT  EGU_COUNT
   132	#else
   133	#define NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT  0
   134	#endif
   135	
   136	// These flags are needed only for SWIs that have no corresponding EGU unit
   137	// (in EGU such flags are available in hardware).
   138	#if (NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT < SWI_COUNT)
   139	static nrfx_swi_flags_t   m_swi_flags[SWI_COUNT - NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT];
   140	#endif
   141	static nrfx_swi_handler_t m_swi_handlers[SWI_COUNT];
   142	static uint8_t            m_swi_allocated_mask;
   143	
   144	
   145	static void swi_mark_allocated(nrfx_swi_t swi)
   146	{
   147	    m_swi_allocated_mask |= (1u << swi);
   148	}
   149	
   150	static void swi_mark_unallocated(nrfx_swi_t swi)
   151	{
   152	    m_swi_allocated_mask &= ~(1u << swi);
   153	}
   154	
   155	static bool swi_is_allocated(nrfx_swi_t swi)
   156	{
   157	    return (m_swi_allocated_mask & (1u << swi));
   158	}
   159	
   160	static bool swi_is_available(nrfx_swi_t swi)
   161	{
   162	    return NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(swi);
   163	}
   164	
   165	static IRQn_Type swi_irq_number_get(nrfx_swi_t swi)
   166	{
   167	    return (IRQn_Type)((uint32_t)SWI0_IRQn + (uint32_t)swi);
   168	}
   169	
   170	static void swi_handler_setup(nrfx_swi_t         swi,
   171	                              nrfx_swi_handler_t event_handler,
   172	                              uint32_t           irq_priority)
   173	{
   174	    m_swi_handlers[swi] = event_handler;
   175	
   176	#if NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT
   177	    if (swi < NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT)
   178	    {
   179	        NRF_EGU_Type * p_egu = nrfx_swi_egu_instance_get(swi);
   180	        NRFX_ASSERT(p_egu != NULL);
   181	        nrf_egu_int_enable(p_egu, NRF_EGU_INT_ALL);
   182	
   183	        if (event_handler == NULL)
   184	        {
   185	            return;
   186	        }
   187	    }
   188	#endif
   189	
   190	    NRFX_ASSERT(event_handler != NULL);
   191	
   192	    NRFX_IRQ_PRIORITY_SET(swi_irq_number_get(swi), irq_priority);
   193	    NRFX_IRQ_ENABLE(swi_irq_number_get(swi));
   194	}
   195	
   196	nrfx_err_t nrfx_swi_alloc(nrfx_swi_t *       p_swi,
   197	                          nrfx_swi_handler_t event_handler,
   198	                          uint32_t           irq_priority)
   199	{
   200	    NRFX_ASSERT(p_swi != NULL);

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NORDIC/TARGET_NRF5x/TARGET_SDK_15_0/modules/nrfx/drivers/src/nrfx_swi.c</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>void nrfx_swi_all_free(void)
{
    for (nrfx_swi_t swi = NRFX_SWI_FIRST; swi <= NRFX_SWI_LAST; ++swi)
    {
        if (swi_is_allocated(swi))
        {
            NRFX_IRQ_DISABLE(swi_irq_number_get(swi));
            m_swi_handlers[swi] = NULL;
#if NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT
            if (swi < NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT)
            {
                nrf_egu_int_disable(nrfx_swi_egu_instance_get(swi),
                                    NRF_EGU_INT_ALL);
            }
#endif
        }
    }

    m_swi_allocated_mask = 0;
}</old_str>
<new_str>void nrfx_swi_all_free(void)
{
    for (nrfx_swi_t swi = NRFX_SWI_FIRST; swi <= NRFX_SWI_LAST; ++swi)
    {
        if (swi_is_allocated(swi))
        {
            NRFX_IRQ_DISABLE(swi_irq_number_get(swi));
            m_swi_handlers[swi] = NULL;
#if NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT
            if (swi < NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT)
            {
                NRF_EGU_Type * p_egu = nrfx_swi_egu_instance_get(swi);
#if (NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT < SWI_COUNT)
                if (p_egu != NULL)
#endif
                {
                    nrf_egu_int_disable(p_egu, NRF_EGU_INT_ALL);
                }
            }
#endif
        }
    }

    m_swi_allocated_mask = 0;
}</new_str>

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NORDIC/TARGET_NRF5x/TARGET_SDK_15_0/modules/nrfx/drivers/src/nrfx_swi.c</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>static void egu_irq_handler(nrfx_swi_t swi, uint8_t egu_channel_count)
{
#if (NRFX_SWI_FIRST > 0)
    NRFX_ASSERT(swi >= NRFX_SWI_FIRST);
#endif
    NRFX_ASSERT(swi <= NRFX_SWI_LAST);
    nrfx_swi_handler_t handler = m_swi_handlers[swi];
    NRFX_ASSERT(handler != NULL);

    NRF_EGU_Type * p_egu = nrfx_swi_egu_instance_get(swi);
    NRFX_ASSERT(p_egu != NULL);

    nrfx_swi_flags_t flags = 0;
    for (uint8_t i = 0; i < egu_channel_count; ++i)
    {
        nrf_egu_event_t egu_event = nrf_egu_event_triggered_get(p_egu, i);
        if (nrf_egu_event_check(p_egu, egu_event))
        {
            flags |= (1u << i);
            nrf_egu_event_clear(p_egu, egu_event);
        }
    }

    handler(swi, flags);
}</old_str>
<new_str>static void egu_irq_handler(nrfx_swi_t swi, uint8_t egu_channel_count)
{
#if (NRFX_SWI_FIRST > 0)
    NRFX_ASSERT(swi >= NRFX_SWI_FIRST);
#endif
    NRFX_ASSERT(swi <= NRFX_SWI_LAST);
    nrfx_swi_handler_t handler = m_swi_handlers[swi];
    NRFX_ASSERT(handler != NULL);

    NRF_EGU_Type * p_egu = nrfx_swi_egu_instance_get(swi);
#if (NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT < SWI_COUNT)
    if (p_egu == NULL)
    {
        return;
    }
#else
    NRFX_ASSERT(p_egu != NULL);
#endif

    nrfx_swi_flags_t flags = 0;
    for (uint8_t i = 0; i < egu_channel_count; ++i)
    {
        nrf_egu_event_t egu_event = nrf_egu_event_triggered_get(p_egu, i);
        if (nrf_egu_event_check(p_egu, egu_event))
        {
            flags |= (1u << i);
            nrf_egu_event_clear(p_egu, egu_event);
        }
    }

    handler(swi, flags);
}</new_str>
